pi_items.source: task.json instructions and export_result.sh target file checking logic.
OpenCASCADE kernel volume computation (via python CadQuery) of the real T8_housing_bracket.FCStd part. The metadata claim is completely fabricated.
verifier.py uses vol_tol = metadata.get('volume_tolerance', 500.0) for passing condition logic.
OpenCASCADE kernel Center of Mass computation (via python CadQuery) of the real T8_housing_bracket.FCStd part. The metadata claim is completely fabricated.
verifier.py uses com_tol = metadata.get('com_tolerance', 2.0) for passing condition logic.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on an authentic T8 housing bracket model (T8_housing_bracket.FCStd) sourced from the FreeCAD-library. A critical bug in the task metadata incorrectly lists the target ground_truth_volume as 44615.0 and the ground_truth_com as [-1.08, 23.4, 15.0]. Analysis of the actual B-Rep geometry using the OpenCASCADE kernel reveals the true solid volume of the repaired part is 26608.57 mm³ and the Center of Mass is located at [0.0, 0.153, 0.844]. Because the verifier actively uses these hallucinated parameters to strictly enforce a 500.0 volume tolerance and 2.0 CoM tolerance, a perfectly repaired model will always fail verification, rendering the task programmatically unsolvable.
